summary: High‑energy set with killer Slipknot! jam and standout Black Peter
blurb: The show is a nonstop blaze of energy, highlighted by a blistering Slipknot! > Franklin's Tower sequence and a fiery Black Peter. Jerry’s guitar riffs are on point while Phil lays down a bottom‑heavy bass foundation. The second set flows perfectly into an inspired Eyes Of The World and ends with a feel‑good Touch of Grey encore.
review: April 17, 1984 at the Niagara Falls Convention Center captured one of the spring ’84 road‑warrior peaks. From the opening “Jack Straw” through a tight first set, the band built momentum that exploded in the second set: the Help On The Way → Slipknot! → Franklin's Tower medley was described as “mind‑blowing,” and the ensuing Women Are Smarter → Eyes Of The World jam kept the crowd dancing. Black Peter received universal praise for its vocal intensity and lyrical delivery, while the drum/space segment was called “fantastic.” Throughout, Jerry’s guitar work stayed sharp—big smile, big riffs—even if his voice was a little rough early in the second set, it quickly settled. Phil’s bass was “rattling all night,” Brent shone on keys, and both Mickey and Bill drove an unstoppable rhythmic pulse that carried the band to a triumphant Touch of Grey encore.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All seven recordings are marked positive, repeatedly praising the Slipknot! jam, Black Peter, Help → Slipknot! → Franklin's Tower sequence and overall high energy. No negative comments appear, indicating strong consensus of an exceptional performance.

best_recording.reason: Pristine soundboard capture with crystal‑clear, flawless audio – the clearest representation of this high‑energy performance.

key_highlights: Blistering Slipknot! > Franklin's Tower sequence that drives the second set.
Fire‑breathing Black Peter with powerful vocals and guitar work.
Eyes Of The World jam following Women Are Smarter, highlighted by a stellar drum/space segment.
Consistently excellent soundboard recordings; the Miller SBD is especially pristine.

band_performance.Jerry: Delivered strong, energetic guitar work with big riffs and a smiling stage presence; vocals were a bit rough at the start of the second set but tightened quickly.
band_performance.Phil: Provided a bottom‑heavy, rattling bass that called the band forward, maintaining solid foundation throughout.
band_performance.Bob: Kept steady rhythm duties and even stepped offstage briefly during the Women Are Smarter jam, showing flexible involvement.
band_performance.Brent: Showcased genial keyboard work, adding bright textures and melodic fills that lifted the second‑set jams.
band_performance.Mickey: Along with Bill delivered a fantastic drum/space segment, driving the improvisational peaks with power and nuance.
band_performance.Billy: Tight drumming throughout, supporting the high‑energy flow and punctuating transitions such as Slipknot! into Franklin's Tower.
